summary: auth check must surface a credential-resolution failure as a diagnostic (stderr line plus a credential_error JSON field) and report both username and password sources as none, instead of silently discarding the error, while still exiting zero.
reason: auth check is a non-failing diagnostic command; a failing --username-command/--password-command or 1Password fetch must not abort it, but the operator needs to know resolution failed. Resolve may return early before defaulting unset sources, leaving UsernameSource empty rather than 'none', so the command must normalize both sources after an error. The resolution error carries only the command exit status, never the secret stdout, so it is safe to print.
validated_outcome: go build/vet/test ./... pass; a failing credential command leaves auth check exit zero, writes 'credential resolution failed: ...' to stderr, emits credential_error and password_source/username_source 'none' in JSON, and never leaks the command's secret stdout.
